what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note (a comment) in which Tim responds to another item, saying a proposed annotation split is good but insisting a general, unrestricted annotation type should still exist.
observations: YAML frontmatter follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type=note, source=claude_code, state=posted, title 'Tim · paragraph', author_session=tim, channel=dragnet-development.

The body is a short paragraph of design feedback: it accepts a prior argument about splitting annotation types, then argues for keeping a general one because Tim's input is not always neat/clean and he does not want to be limited.
It mentions 'giving text input' as a reason a general type may be workable.
standouts: Tim explicitly pushes back for a general/open-ended annotation category despite agreeing a split is useful.
Strong emphasis on not wanting to be 'limited or restricted' in how he annotates.
It is a threaded comment on item-8c087208 in the dragnet-development channel.
conventions: Follows the standard noticeboard item format: YAML metadata, markdown body, history array, typed links.
Uses a 'commented_on' link to relate the note to its parent item.
concerns: The phrase 'A lot of what I want is necessarily neat or clean' is ambiguous — likely a typo for 'not necessarily neat or clean', which changes the meaning.
The note is terse and assumes the reader already knows the 'split' being discussed.
notes: Tim's feedback note on an annotation taxonomy proposal: keep the split, but also keep a general, unrestricted annotation type.
questions: What exact annotation categories or 'split' is being discussed?
What is the content of item-8c087208?
What does 'giving text input' refer to — a UI field, a command, or a data format?
Does 'necessarily neat or clean' mean 'not necessarily neat or clean'?
What would the behavior of the requested 'general one' be relative to the specific categories?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is labeled as a note/comment and its body is exactly a feedback comment.
apparent_themes: User freedom and avoiding restrictive workflows
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to preserve expressive freedom in an annotation system by ensuring there is a catch-all category for messy or unspecified intent, while not rejecting more specific categories entirely.
novelty: It is a direct user-level resistance to over-categorization, explicitly requesting an unconstrained 'general' annotation type alongside more specific ones.
contribution: Captures a design constraint that a fused system would keep: the need for an open-ended, general annotation type to accommodate ambiguous or non-categorized user intent, recorded as feedback on a specific proposal.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim in dragnet-development commenting on item-8c087208, accepting the value of an annotation split while arguing that a general, unrestricted text-input annotation type must still exist.
intention_for_embedding: To record Tim's design feedback advocating for an open-ended annotation category alongside more specific annotation kinds.
